desc: Toledo crowns a hill above the Tagus River and preserves one of Spain’s richest medieval cityscapes. Known as the “City of Three Cultures,” it offers layered Christian, Jewish, and Islamic heritage in a dense maze of stone streets.
hls: Toledo Cathedral and El Greco heritage
Traditional sword and damascene craft legacy

todo.name: Toledo Cathedral interior tour
todo.desc: Explore one of Spain’s greatest Gothic cathedrals, including treasury and choir details.

todo.name: Alcázar and Army Museum
todo.desc: Visit the dominant hilltop fortress for military collections and panoramic terraces.

todo.name: Jewish Quarter and synagogues
todo.desc: Walk medieval lanes and key sites like Santa María la Blanca and El Tránsito.

todo.name: El Greco Museum and church stops
todo.desc: Understand the painter’s Toledo years through collections and related city churches.

todo.name: Mirador del Valle viewpoint
todo.desc: Cross the river for the classic postcard panorama of Toledo’s skyline on the ridge.

todo.name: Marzipan and local tavern tasting
todo.desc: Sample Toledo marzipan, game dishes, and Castilian wines in old-town taverns.

wifi.note: Reliable in historic-center hotels and cafés, though old stone buildings can reduce in-room signal strength.

prac.fltNote: Toledo has no airport; fly to Madrid-Barajas (MAD) then continue by high-speed train (~35 minutes from Madrid Atocha).
prac.visa: Spain is in Schengen; many visitors (including US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.
alerts: Steep cobbled streets and summer heat can make midday walking demanding.

sweet: April to June and September to October are better for hilltop walking than midsummer afternoons.
